exercises.title: B: Lucky accidents
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.passage:
Some discoveries happen when people (9) ...... trying to solve a different problem. Sometimes inventions (10) ...... created by chance, not because anyone planned them.

Percy Spencer, (11) ...... worked with radar equipment, was standing (12) ...... his laboratory when he noticed that (13) ...... chocolate bar in his pocket had melted. He realised that the machine (14) ...... heat food very quickly.

Another example is Velcro, (15) ...... was inspired by small seeds that stuck to clothes. George de Mestral was walking (16) ...... the countryside when he noticed that the seeds (17) ...... attached themselves to his dog's fur. He studied them and saw that they (18) ...... be used to make a useful fastener.

gapExplanationContent.whyCorrect: "are" is the only word that completes this gap in the passage.
gapExplanationContent.commonMistake: Read the whole line in the passage — tense, preposition, or word form must match the surrounding context.
gapExplanationContent.usefulTip: Read the sentence before and after the gap — the story context usually signals tense and word choice.
gapExplanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
My phone isn't working, so I can't call you. **WRONG**
There's ...... my phone, so I can't call you.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: "something wrong with" is the only form that completes the sentence correctly.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "something wrong with" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "My phone isn't working, so I can't call you. WRONG".

exercises.title: D: Word Formation
exercises.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: Good ...... is important when people work in teams. (COMMUNICATE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: From (COMMUNICATE), the correct form here is "communication".

wrongOptions.communicate: The root COMMUNICATE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "communication".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of COMMUNICATE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: COMMUNICATE → communication: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "communication" is the word-formation answer from the root COMMUNICATE in this context.

items.sentence: Be ...... when you cross that busy road. (CARE)

exercises.title: G: Wrong form
exercises.instructions: One word in each sentence is in the wrong form. Write the correct form.
exercises.studentInstruction: One word in each sentence is in the wrong form. Write the correct form.

items.displayPrompt: Stop inventing stories! You're such a <s>lie</s>.

cues: Stop inventing stories! You're such a <s>lie<

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The word "lie" is incorrect in this context — use "liar" to complete: Stop inventing stories! You're such a "liar".
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Replace "lie" with "liar" to complete the sentence correctly.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Replace the incorrect word "lie" with the correct form "liar".

wrongOptions.lie: "lie" is the wrong form here — the correct word is "liar".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Do not leave "lie" — use the correct form "liar" for this sentence pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the whole sentence — the highlighted word must match the grammar and meaning of the context.

items.displayPrompt: This sofa is really <s>comfortable</s>; I can't sit on it for long.

explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Replace "comfortable" with "uncomfortable" to complete the sentence correctly.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prefixes change meaning — un-/dis-/in- often make the opposite of the base word.
